acceptance_criteria: endpoint_adjudication_4.xlsx is parseable by openpyxl in the verifier environment (deterministic_precheck pass).
expert_memo.md includes per-row rationale for committee review flags and a reproducibility note.

Grader/verifier outputs contain consistent statements about semantic evaluation (either memo-only with semantic_score null, or programmatic verification with semantic_score documented and evidence refs).
A verification script/runlog demonstrating programmatic reading of the xlsx/CSV is included in the attempt artifacts.
extra_model_fields.priority_justification: Addresses reproducibility and scoring-consistency issues that affect external verification and downstream automation.

planned_changes: Regenerate endpoint_adjudication_4.xlsx using a stable, widely-supported library (openpyxl or XlsxWriter) or include a corresponding CSV with explicit cell-coordinate annotations. Validate in the local environment that openpyxl can parse the workbook.
Embed explicit cell-level citations in the spreadsheet (e.g., Input_Records!B2=40) and include the remediation formulas as real Excel formulas in the Remediation_Calcs sheet, not only in the memo.

Update expert_memo.md to: (a) add a one-line rationale for each row flagged needs_review_committee_review_required; (b) explicitly state whether semantic checks were performed using memo preview only or were corroborated by programmatic parsing of the xlsx; (c) include a short reproducibility note listing required libraries and versions.

Fix grader/verifier output production to be internally consistent: if semantic checks remain memo-only, remove nested semantic_score claims or set semantic_score=null and document the basis; if programmatic checks are added, include the parsing evidence (parsing logs or deterministic_precheck details) and set semantic_score accordingly.

Add a minimal verification script in the package that reads the xlsx/CSV and asserts that computed formula results match the memo's reported intermediates; include its output in the revised attempt's trace or final_message.txt.
Run a local deterministic precheck demonstrating that the spreadsheet is parseable; include the precheck output in the attempt artifacts so automated graders/verifiers can validate programmatically.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve reproducibility and resolve internal consistency in grading outputs by providing a programmatically-parsable spreadsheet (or CSV) and clarifying semantic-evaluation claims.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and traceable; grader awarded full artifact-contract score (1.0). Two issues reduce reproducibility and internal consistency: (1) the grader outputs contain inconsistent semantic-score claims (top-level deterministic_precheck indicates semantic correctness was not programmatically evaluated while nested raw outputs claim semantic_score=1.0); (2) the spreadsheet could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so semantic validation relied on the expert_memo.md preview only. Both are actionable to improve reproducibility and reviewer confidence.
